golden_exemplars.content:
Subject: Escalating — Integration & Data Setup is now 34 days past its expected date

The Integration & Data Setup phase was due Aug 14 and hasn't closed; that's 34 days past expected, which is why this is an escalation rather than another status check.

Where things actually stand: 3 of 5 scoped milestones are complete (asset data load, driver roster import, Live Map activation). The remaining 2 — Dispatch Bridge connector validation and Route Optimizer configuration — have been blocked on the same open item since Aug 20: we're waiting on read access to your dispatch system's event log so we can isolate why acknowledgements are dropping.

This is a shared operational problem, not a reflection on your team — but the timeline slip is real, and 34 days is past the point where another routine follow-up is the right move.

What I'm asking for: a named owner on your side for the event-log access request, with a date, by end of this week. If that owner and date can't be confirmed by Friday, I'd like to bring in your account's executive sponsor for a short call next week to unblock it.

I'm not asking you to commit to a new go-live date today — once we have the log access, FleetOps will come back with a real one.

Marcus Webb
Customer Success Manager, FleetOps Platform
golden_exemplars.title: Activation-gap escalation, past 30 days — the FleetOps house style

output.customer_draft:
Subject: Aligning an owner on your FleetOps rollout

Hi Jordan,

We've noticed a drop in weekly active users on your FleetOps Platform account, and there's currently no assigned owner on the success plan to drive the next steps. Before that gap starts to affect your rollout timeline, I'd like to get the right person on your side aligned so we can keep momentum.

I'd suggest a short working session to review the adoption gap and agree on who owns the next activation step. If you're open to it, you can grab a time here: Schedule a working session (https://calendar.example/schedule/fleetops-csm-working-session).

Let me know what works and we'll take it from there.

Best regards,
FleetOps Customer Success
output.reason: Globex Manufacturing shows a meaningful adoption drop in weekly active users (sig-globex-drop, telemetry, observed 2026-06-21), and the success plan has a missing owner gap (plan-globex-gap, cs_platform, observed 2026-06-22). These two factors drive the deterministic priority score of 82, warranting proposed customer outreach to align on an owner and address the adoption gap.

golden_exemplars.content:
Subject: Recap — Integration & Data Setup sync, Sept 12

The Dispatch Bridge connector is still failing to acknowledge about 12% of outbound dispatch events; that's the one blocker holding this phase up.

What we covered:
- Reviewed the acknowledgement-rate numbers from the last 7 days (88% steady state, target is 99%+ before we call Integration & Data Setup done).
- Walked through the three retry-timeout settings on your side that are the likely cause.
- Agreed the fastest path is a joint session with your IT team rather than another round of one-off tickets.

Action items:
- Northgate: confirm IT availability for a 45-minute working session — owner Teresa Kwan (your side), needed by Sept 16.
- FleetOps: Grace Okafor to prepare a connector-log export isolating the failed acknowledgements — owner Grace Okafor, due Sept 15.
- FleetOps: send the updated retry-timeout recommendation in writing ahead of the working session — owner Marcus Webb, due Sept 15.

Once the working session happens we'll have a clear owner and date for closing this out. Let me know if Sept 16 doesn't work and we'll find another day this week.

Marcus Webb
Customer Success Manager, FleetOps Platform
